extraction.one_line_summary: KITTEL Marcel won Stage 2 with a late surge from wheel-hopping through gaps in final 500m, taking 10th career Tour stage.
tactical_patterns: KITTEL Marcel deliberately abandoned Quick-Step leadout plan at 500m, freelancing wheel-to-wheel through gaps proves superior to structured trains.
KATUSHA's aggressive corner entry while pacing peloton 30km out caused crash hitting FROOME Chris, proving chase teams create self-inflicted danger.
Quick-Step riders racing out-of-contract through Tour creates measurable performance pressure spike, KITTEL demonstrating urgency translates to wins.
THOMAS Geraint in yellow jersey avoided danger by positioning near front, proving jersey magic creates positioning discipline.

rider_signals.KITTEL Marcel: positive: Abandoning leadout structure for instinctive freelancing at 500m reveals peak confidence and could unlock multiple wins.
rider_signals.FROOME Chris: neutral: Positioned correctly 8 riders behind working teams before crash, textbook defense undermined by pure bad luck.
rider_signals.PHINNEY Taylor: positive: Leading 99% of 200km stage in breakaway demonstrates form beyond normal domestique willingness, threatens aggressive racing.
rider_signals.KÜNG Stefan: positive: White jersey retention from TT strength suggests BMC has unexpected GC support depth for PORTE Richie.
rider_signals.DÉMARE Arnaud: positive: Closest challenger to KITTEL despite not winning shows sprint form threatening for remaining flat stages.
rider_signals.CAVENDISH Mark: positive: Fifth place after earlier crash demonstrates crash recovery ability and sprint form durability under damage.
rider_signals.SAGAN Peter: neutral: Looking strongest at 5 seconds remaining but fading to tenth suggests timing mismatch rather than power deficit.
